body_txt: If a creative wants to expand and flesh out a story they are adapting, then directly showing an unseen character can be a way of further developing that character and their relationship with the other characters. If a character has an unseen Abusive Parent in the original work whose treatment of them significantly impacts their life going forward, an adaptation can further develop that relationship and the character by showing the parent and their abuse. However, Tropes Are Tools and sometimes there's a reason the character remained unseen in the original work. In these cases, showing them might detract from the quality of the adaptation. Compare Unseen No More , which is when The Ghost is finally shown within a single work/continuity. This trope might overlap with Named by the Adaptation if the character was unnamed as well as unseen in the original work. Will often also overlap with Suddenly Voiced .
